---
title: "LRP158: Mechanism model — letter-sound (L) → word reading (W), complete-case comparator"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP158 is the **complete-case comparator** for [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058). It is identical in
every respect — same adjustment set, same likelihood, same priors, same sampler — except that the
**mean-imputed rows are dropped**, so every confounder is genuinely observed.

## Why a comparator is needed

The revised-DAG confounders enter LRP58 by the **missing-indicator method**: hearing ($HS$), speech
production ($SP$) and phonological memory ($RW$) are filled to their column mean and a
`{col}_missing` flag carries the unknown group as its own adjustment level. That keeps every child,
which matters at $n \approx 54$.

But preserving rows is not the same as controlling confounding. The method assumes the imputed
group's confounder effect is captured by a **single intercept shift** — that within the "unknown"
stratum the confounder is unrelated to exposure and outcome once the flag is in the model. That is
an assumption, not a property of the method, and it is doing real work here:

| confounder | missing |
| --- | --- |
| hearing (`hearing_c`) | **10 of 54 children, at every wave** (≈ 19%) |
| speech production (`deapp_c`) | ≈ 4% of rows |
| phonological memory (`erbto`) | ≈ 6.5% of rows |
| **complete on all three, every wave** | **35 of 54 children** |

## How to read this model

_A comparator, not a competitor._ If the mechanism slope here **agrees** with LRP58's, the imputation is not driving the result, and
LRP58 stands as the primary (higher-powered) fit.

If they **diverge**, the imputation is load-bearing and *neither* fit should be reported without the
other.

This model is smaller, so its intervals are **wider by construction**. That is the price of the
restriction, not a finding — do not read the wider interval as weaker evidence.

_Convergence._ Like [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058), this model keeps the nonparametric HSGP curve. After the #265
reparameterisation (standardised GP input, a moderate-lengthscale prior, a smaller basis) it clears
the strict zero-divergence gate at `target_accept` = 0.999 with the curve intact — the same fix that
LRP58 carries, so the comparison of slopes between them stays like-for-like.

_Still an adjusted association, not a mechanism._ Latent general ability ($GA$) is **not** adjusted for, and the child random intercept does **not**
stand in for it: a zero-mean, predictor-independent random effect absorbs stable residual
heterogeneity but cannot block $L \leftarrow GA \rightarrow W$. **Residual confounding by $GA$
remains**, so $f^L$ is an **adjusted association**, never a causal effect. The randomised causal
claim lives in the ITT suite.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
